text.title: Django Service Layer Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Django Service Layer application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Service functions accept primitives, dataclasses, or pydantic models — never Django request, view, or queryset objects from the caller.
rule.rationale: Accepting request/queryset couples the service to HTTP and ORM internals; the function becomes untestable in isolation.

rule.statement: Service functions return primitives, dataclasses, or pydantic models — never HttpResponse, Template, or rendered HTML.
rule.rationale: Returning HTTP objects means the service can only be called from views; admin/tasks/CLI must duplicate logic.

rule.statement: Inside service modules, no imports from django.http, rest_framework.response, or template engines.
rule.rationale: HTTP imports inside services are a leaking-abstraction smell; enforce via ruff isort rule + import linter contract.

rule.statement: Database mutations (ORM .save/.delete/.create) happen only inside service functions. Views and admins call services; they do not mutate the ORM directly.
rule.rationale: ORM calls scattered across views means transactional boundaries become accidental; centralising in services lets you wrap operations in atomic blocks deterministically.

rule.statement: One service module per aggregate (orders/, payments/, accounts/). Functions group by aggregate, not by HTTP verb or by caller type.
rule.rationale: Module organised by caller scatters aggregate logic; module organised by aggregate concentrates it for easy reasoning + testing.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
